description: mutation-test a gate — mutate its source, run its test slice, score the survivors
rules: operate on a sandbox COPY of PROJECT_DIR — never the live tree
the baseline TEST_CMD must pass on un-mutated code or the score is rejected as undefined
a surviving mutant is a hole in the gate: it is listed by id in mutate.json
usage: Set PROJECT_DIR (copied to a sandbox), TARGET (file within it to mutate), TEST_CMD (the test slice; nonzero exit = mutant killed). Optional THRESHOLD (default 0.90), MAX_MUTANTS (default 50). Output: mutate.json.
limitation: Single-token operator mutations on Python source (comparisons, boolean ops, +/-, True/False) over the WHOLE target file — not a full AST mutation engine, and not scoped to a function. Against a CLI-bearing module (one with __main__/argparse/print) the score is diluted by mutants in non-gate lines, so point TARGET at the tightest module containing the gate and read the survived[] ids (token@byte-offset) to see exactly which mutants lived. Function/line-range scoping is future work.
